link|Q11523074}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine |Image=[[Image:Muraya Jinja Honden.jpg|270px]]
Torii gate and worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159}} Kurado 426 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 32 | Latitude seconds = 54.4 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 49 | Longitude seconds = 3.3 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Miho-tsu-hime|Q6378698|WD=Q6378698|lt=Miho-tsu-hime no Mikoto}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (Major)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Main hall={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |Alternative name=Muraya Shrine |Founded=Unknown |Map=Japan Nara }} '''Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine''' (むらやにいますみふつひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. The shrine stands facing the “Nakatsu-michi” (Tachibana Highway), one of the {{ill|lt=ancient roads of Yamato|Q11433382|WD=Q11433382|lt=Three Yamato Roads}}. The principal enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Miho-tsu-hime|Q6378698|WD=Q6378698|lt=Miho-tsu-hime no Mikoto}} (also known as Mifutsuhime-gami), with {{ill|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi no Mikoto}} enshrined as an accompanying deity. Miho-tsu-hime no Mikoto is the consort deity of {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi no Mikoto}}, and in the myths recorded in the *Kojiki* and *Nihon Shoki*, Ōmononushi and Ōkuninushi are regarded as the same deity. Ōmononushi no Mikoto is the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542}}, and this shrine, which enshrines his consort deity, is considered a branch shrine of Ōmiwa Shrine. ===History=== === Overview === The date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. According to the *{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}*, during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252}} in the first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}’s reign ({{ill|lt=673|Q31105|WD=Q31105}}), the Muraya deity delivered a {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123}} to {{ill|lt=Ōtomo no Fukei|Q11432333|WD=Q11432333}}, who was on the side of Prince Ōama, saying, “The enemy is coming, so block the Nakatsu-michi.” In recognition of this service, a {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} was conferred upon the deity. This is the earliest recorded instance of the bestowal of a divine rank. Records such as the Yamato Province Tax Register ({{ill|lt=Shōsōin documents|Q11545362|WD=Q11545362}}) and the {{ill|lt=Shinshōkyakuchokufu-shō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312}} note that the imperial court made repeated donations of {{ill|lt=shrine estates|Q1689534|WD=Q1689534}} to the shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, the shrine is listed as a major shrine and is recorded as receiving offerings at the Tsukinami, Aename, and Niiname festivals. In Shōwa 2 ({{ill|lt=1926|Q5259|WD=Q5259}}), the shrine was elevated to the rank of prefectural shrine. {{quotation| '''“Emperor Tenmu, Upper Volume,” *Nihon Shoki, Lower Volume*, Kodansha Academic Library'''
“Then the deity Muraya (Moriya Shrine) possessed the priest (shinkan) and said, ‘An army will come along the road within my shrine grounds. Therefore, block the shrine road.’” }} === Divine Ranks === * {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japanese era)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (*{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}*) – Recorded as “Muraya Nifutsuhime-gami.” === Shrine Grounds === File:Muraya Jinja Torii1.jpg|First torii gate File:Muraya Jinja Torii2.jpg|Second torii gate File:Muraya Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall === Subsidiary Shrines === ;Sessha Muraya Shrine (むらやじんじゃ) : A Shikinaisha. Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi no Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime no Ōkami}}, {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi no Kami}}, as well as Ōtomo no Takemochi no Muraji and Muroya no Muraji. : Formerly located on the riverside east of the current site at the place name Miyanoyama, but it was destroyed by warfare in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 12 and subsequently relocated to the present shrine grounds. ;Massha Hattori Shrine (はっとりじんじゃ) : A Shikinaisha. Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|Q2842779|WD=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mihoko|Q119380365|WD=Q119380365|lt=Ame-no-Mihoko no Mikoto}}. : Formerly located at Kakimori in Ōaza Daianji, it was destroyed by warfare during the Nanboku-chō period and relocated to the present shrine grounds in Tenshō 1 ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678}}). ;Sessha Kusumi Shrine (Wakamiya Ebisu Shrine) (くすすみじんじゃ) : A Shikinaisha. Enshrined deities: Ame-no-Kunohi no Mikoto and {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto}}. : Formerly located on the riverside east of the current site at Ebisu-yama, it was relocated to the present shrine grounds in Keiō 2. ;Massha Ichikishimahime Shrine (いちきしまひめじんじゃ) and Mononobe Shrine (もののべじんじゃ) : Enshrined deities: Kashiwaya-hime no Mikoto and Umashimaji no Mikoto; accompanying enshrinement of Mononobe no Moriya no Muraji. Their origins are unknown, but they are said to enshrine the ancestral deities of the shrine families, particularly the Moriya family. File:Muraya Jinja Sessha1.jpg|Muraya Shrine File:Muraya Jinja Massha1.jpg|Hattori Shrine File:Muraya Jinja Sessha2.jpg|Kusumi Shrine (Wakamiya Ebisu Shrine) File:Muraya Jinja Massha2.jpg|Mononobe Shrine (Ichikishimahime Shrine) === Access === * About a 30-minute walk west from {{ill|lt=Makimuku Station|Q4980794|WD=Q4980794}} on the {{ill|lt=Sakurai Line|Q865801|WD=Q865801}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}} * About a 40-minute walk east from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|WD=Q7689224}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related Articles === * {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736}} === External Links === {{Commonscat|Muraya-jinja}} * [http://www.murayajinja.com Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/temple/3444.html Muraya Shrine] – Tawaramoto Town * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22803 Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Compilation” {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Murayaniimasumifutsuhimejinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Subshrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大むらやにますみふつひめしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]]

link|Q11523074}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine |Image=[[Image:Muraya Jinja Honden.jpg|270px]]
Torii gate and worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159}} Kurado 426 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 32 | Latitude seconds = 54.4 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 49 | Longitude seconds = 3.3 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Miho-tsu-hime|Q6378698|WD=Q6378698|lt=Miho-tsu-hime no Mikoto}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (Major)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Main hall={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |Alternative name=Muraya Shrine |Founded=Unknown |Map=Japan Nara }} '''Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine''' (むらやにいますみふつひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. The shrine stands facing the “Nakatsu-michi” (Tachibana Highway), one of the {{ill|lt=ancient roads of Yamato|Q11433382|WD=Q11433382|lt=Three Yamato Roads}}. The principal enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Miho-tsu-hime|Q6378698|WD=Q6378698|lt=Miho-tsu-hime no Mikoto}} (also known as Mifutsuhime-gami), with {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi no Mikoto}} enshrined as an accompanying deity. Miho-tsu-hime no Mikoto is the consort deity of {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi no Mikoto}}, and in the myths recorded in the *Kojiki* and *Nihon Shoki*, Ōmononushi and Ōkuninushi are regarded as the same deity. Ōmononushi no Mikoto is the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542}}, and this shrine, which enshrines his consort deity, is considered a branch shrine of Ōmiwa Shrine. ===History=== === Overview === The date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. According to the *{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}*, during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252}} in the first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}’s reign ({{ill|lt=673|Q31105|WD=Q31105}}), the Muraya deity delivered a {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123}} to {{ill|lt=Ōtomo no Fukei|Q11432333|WD=Q11432333}}, who was on the side of Prince Ōama, saying, “The enemy is coming, so block the Nakatsu-michi.” In recognition of this service, a {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} was conferred upon the deity. This is the earliest recorded instance of the bestowal of a divine rank. Records such as the Yamato Province Tax Register ({{ill|lt=Shōsōin documents|Q11545362|WD=Q11545362}}) and the {{ill|lt=Shinshōkyakuchokufu-shō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312}} note that the imperial court made repeated donations of {{ill|lt=shrine estates|Q1689534|WD=Q1689534}} to the shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, the shrine is listed as a major shrine and is recorded as receiving offerings at the Tsukinami, Aename, and Niiname festivals. In Shōwa 2 ({{ill|lt=1926|Q5259|WD=Q5259}}), the shrine was elevated to the rank of prefectural shrine. {{quotation| '''“Emperor Tenmu, Upper Volume,” *Nihon Shoki, Lower Volume*, Kodansha Academic Library'''
“Then the deity Muraya (Moriya Shrine) possessed the priest (shinkan) and said, ‘An army will come along the road within my shrine grounds. Therefore, block the shrine road.’” }} === Divine Ranks === * {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japanese era)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (*{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}*) – Recorded as “Muraya Nifutsuhime-gami.” === Shrine Grounds === File:Muraya Jinja Torii1.jpg|First torii gate File:Muraya Jinja Torii2.jpg|Second torii gate File:Muraya Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall === Subsidiary Shrines === ;Sessha Muraya Shrine (むらやじんじゃ) : A Shikinaisha. Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi no Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime no Ōkami}}, {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi no Kami}}, as well as Ōtomo no Takemochi no Muraji and Muroya no Muraji. : Formerly located on the riverside east of the current site at the place name Miyanoyama, but it was destroyed by warfare in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 12 and subsequently relocated to the present shrine grounds. ;Massha Hattori Shrine (はっとりじんじゃ) : A Shikinaisha. Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|Q2842779|WD=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mihoko|Q119380365|WD=Q119380365|lt=Ame-no-Mihoko no Mikoto}}. : Formerly located at Kakimori in Ōaza Daianji, it was destroyed by warfare during the Nanboku-chō period and relocated to the present shrine grounds in Tenshō 1 ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678}}). ;Sessha Kusumi Shrine (Wakamiya Ebisu Shrine) (くすすみじんじゃ) : A Shikinaisha. Enshrined deities: Ame-no-Kunohi no Mikoto and {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto}}. : Formerly located on the riverside east of the current site at Ebisu-yama, it was relocated to the present shrine grounds in Keiō 2. ;Massha Ichikishimahime Shrine (いちきしまひめじんじゃ) and Mononobe Shrine (もののべじんじゃ) : Enshrined deities: Kashiwaya-hime no Mikoto and Umashimaji no Mikoto; accompanying enshrinement of Mononobe no Moriya no Muraji. Their origins are unknown, but they are said to enshrine the ancestral deities of the shrine families, particularly the Moriya family. File:Muraya Jinja Sessha1.jpg|Muraya Shrine File:Muraya Jinja Massha1.jpg|Hattori Shrine File:Muraya Jinja Sessha2.jpg|Kusumi Shrine (Wakamiya Ebisu Shrine) File:Muraya Jinja Massha2.jpg|Mononobe Shrine (Ichikishimahime Shrine) === Access === * About a 30-minute walk west from {{ill|lt=Makimuku Station|Q4980794|WD=Q4980794}} on the {{ill|lt=Sakurai Line|Q865801|WD=Q865801}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}} * About a 40-minute walk east from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|WD=Q7689224}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related Articles === * {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736}} === External Links === {{Commonscat|Muraya-jinja}} * [http://www.murayajinja.com Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/temple/3444.html Muraya Shrine] – Tawaramoto Town * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22803 Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Compilation” {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Murayaniimasumifutsuhimejinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Subshrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大むらやにますみふつひめしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]]

link|Q11523074}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine |Image=[[Image:Muraya Jinja Honden.jpg|270px]]
Torii gate and worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159}} Kurado 426 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 32 | Latitude seconds = 54.4 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 49 | Longitude seconds = 3.3 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Miho-tsu-hime|Q6378698|WD=Q6378698|lt=Miho-tsu-hime no Mikoto}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (Major)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Main hall={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |Alternative name=Muraya Shrine |Founded=Unknown |Map=Japan Nara }} '''Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine''' (むらやにいますみふつひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. The shrine stands facing the “Nakatsu-michi” (Tachibana Highway), one of the {{ill|lt=ancient roads of Yamato|Q11433382|WD=Q11433382|lt=Three Yamato Roads}}. The principal enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Miho-tsu-hime|Q6378698|WD=Q6378698|lt=Miho-tsu-hime no Mikoto}} (also known as Mifutsuhime-gami), with {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi no Mikoto}} enshrined as an accompanying deity. Miho-tsu-hime no Mikoto is the consort deity of {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi no Mikoto}}, and in the myths recorded in the *Kojiki* and *Nihon Shoki*, Ōmononushi and Ōkuninushi are regarded as the same deity. Ōmononushi no Mikoto is the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542}}, and this shrine, which enshrines his consort deity, is considered a branch shrine of Ōmiwa Shrine. ===History=== === Overview === The date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. According to the *{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935}}*, during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252}} in the first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181}}’s reign ({{ill|lt=673|Q31105|WD=Q31105}}), the Muraya deity delivered a {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123}} to {{ill|lt=Ōtomo no Fukei|Q11432333|WD=Q11432333}}, who was on the side of Prince Ōama, saying, “The enemy is coming, so block the Nakatsu-michi.” In recognition of this service, a {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} was conferred upon the deity. This is the earliest recorded instance of the bestowal of a divine rank. Records such as the Yamato Province Tax Register ({{ill|lt=Shōsōin documents|Q11545362|WD=Q11545362}}) and the {{ill|lt=Shinshōkyakuchokufu-shō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312}} note that the imperial court made repeated donations of {{ill|lt=shrine estates|Q1689534|WD=Q1689534}} to the shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, the shrine is listed as a major shrine and is recorded as receiving offerings at the Tsukinami, Aename, and Niiname festivals. In Shōwa 2 ({{ill|lt=1926|Q5259|WD=Q5259}}), the shrine was elevated to the rank of prefectural shrine. {{quotation| '''“Emperor Tenmu, Upper Volume,” *Nihon Shoki, Lower Volume*, Kodansha Academic Library'''
“Then the deity Muraya (Moriya Shrine) possessed the priest (shinkan) and said, ‘An army will come along the road within my shrine grounds. Therefore, block the shrine road.’” }} === Divine Ranks === * {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japanese era)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284}}: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (*{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}*) – Recorded as “Muraya Nifutsuhime-gami.” === Shrine Grounds === File:Muraya Jinja Torii1.jpg|First torii gate File:Muraya Jinja Torii2.jpg|Second torii gate File:Muraya Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall === Subsidiary Shrines === ;Sessha Muraya Shrine (むらやじんじゃ) : A Shikinaisha. Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi no Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime no Ōkami}}, {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi no Kami}}, as well as Ōtomo no Takemochi no Muraji and Muroya no Muraji. : Formerly located on the riverside east of the current site at the place name Miyanoyama, but it was destroyed by warfare in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} 12 and subsequently relocated to the present shrine grounds. ;Massha Hattori Shrine (はっとりじんじゃ) : A Shikinaisha. Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|Q2842779|WD=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mihoko|Q119380365|WD=Q119380365|lt=Ame-no-Mihoko no Mikoto}}. : Formerly located at Kakimori in Ōaza Daianji, it was destroyed by warfare during the Nanboku-chō period and relocated to the present shrine grounds in Tenshō 1 ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678}}). ;Sessha Kusumi Shrine (Wakamiya Ebisu Shrine) (くすすみじんじゃ) : A Shikinaisha. Enshrined deities: Ame-no-Kunohi no Mikoto and {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto}}. : Formerly located on the riverside east of the current site at Ebisu-yama, it was relocated to the present shrine grounds in Keiō 2. ;Massha Ichikishimahime Shrine (いちきしまひめじんじゃ) and Mononobe Shrine (もののべじんじゃ) : Enshrined deities: Kashiwaya-hime no Mikoto and Umashimaji no Mikoto; accompanying enshrinement of Mononobe no Moriya no Muraji. Their origins are unknown, but they are said to enshrine the ancestral deities of the shrine families, particularly the Moriya family. File:Muraya Jinja Sessha1.jpg|Muraya Shrine File:Muraya Jinja Massha1.jpg|Hattori Shrine File:Muraya Jinja Sessha2.jpg|Kusumi Shrine (Wakamiya Ebisu Shrine) File:Muraya Jinja Massha2.jpg|Mononobe Shrine (Ichikishimahime Shrine) === Access === * About a 30-minute walk west from {{ill|lt=Makimuku Station|Q4980794|WD=Q4980794}} on the {{ill|lt=Sakurai Line|Q865801|WD=Q865801}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}} * About a 40-minute walk east from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|WD=Q7689224}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related Articles === * {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736}} === External Links === {{Commonscat|Muraya-jinja}} * [http://www.murayajinja.com Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/temple/3444.html Muraya Shrine] – Tawaramoto Town * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22803 Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Compilation” {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Murayaniimasumifutsuhimejinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Subshrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大むらやにますみふつひめしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Muraya-jinja}} [[JA:村屋坐弥冨都比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523074}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine |Image=[[Image:Muraya Jinja Honden.jpg|270px]]
Torii gate and worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}} Kurado 426 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 32 | Latitude seconds = 54.4 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 49 | Longitude seconds = 3.3 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Miho-tsu-hime|Q6378698|WD=Q6378698|lt=Miho-tsu-hime no Mikoto|1=Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Major)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Main hall={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |Alternative name=Muraya Shrine |Founded=Unknown |Map=Japan Nara }} '''Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine''' (むらやにいますみふつひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. The shrine stands facing the “Nakatsu-michi” (Tachibana Highway), one of the {{ill|lt=ancient roads of Yamato|Q11433382|WD=Q11433382|lt=Three Yamato Roads|1=Three Yamato Roads}}. The principal enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Miho-tsu-hime|Q6378698|WD=Q6378698|lt=Miho-tsu-hime no Mikoto|1=Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto}} (also known as Mifutsuhime-gami), with {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi no Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} enshrined as an accompanying deity. Miho-tsu-hime no Mikoto is the consort deity of {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi no Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}, and in the myths recorded in the *Kojiki* and *Nihon Shoki*, Ōmononushi and Ōkuninushi are regarded as the same deity. Ōmononushi no Mikoto is the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}}, and this shrine, which enshrines his consort deity, is considered a branch shrine of Ōmiwa Shrine. ===History=== === Overview === The date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. According to the *{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}*, during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}} in the first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}’s reign ({{ill|lt=673|Q31105|WD=Q31105|1=673}}), the Muraya deity delivered a {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|1=Oracle}} to {{ill|lt=Ōtomo no Fukei|Q11432333|WD=Q11432333|1=Ōtomo no Fukei}}, who was on the side of Prince Ōama, saying, “The enemy is coming, so block the Nakatsu-michi.” In recognition of this service, a {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} was conferred upon the deity. This is the earliest recorded instance of the bestowal of a divine rank. Records such as the Yamato Province Tax Register ({{ill|lt=Shōsōin documents|Q11545362|WD=Q11545362|1=Shōsōin Documents}}) and the {{ill|lt=Shinshōkyakuchokufu-shō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}} note that the imperial court made repeated donations of {{ill|lt=shrine estates|Q1689534|WD=Q1689534|1=Shinpo}} to the shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, the shrine is listed as a major shrine and is recorded as receiving offerings at the Tsukinami, Aename, and Niiname festivals. In Shōwa 2 ({{ill|lt=1926|Q5259|WD=Q5259|1=1926}}), the shrine was elevated to the rank of prefectural shrine. {{quotation| '''“Emperor Tenmu, Upper Volume,” *Nihon Shoki, Lower Volume*, Kodansha Academic Library'''
“Then the deity Muraya (Moriya Shrine) possessed the priest (shinkan) and said, ‘An army will come along the road within my shrine grounds. Therefore, block the shrine road.’” }} === Divine Ranks === * {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japanese era)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (*{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}*) – Recorded as “Muraya Nifutsuhime-gami.” === Shrine Grounds === File:Muraya Jinja Torii1.jpg|First torii gate File:Muraya Jinja Torii2.jpg|Second torii gate File:Muraya Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall === Subsidiary Shrines === ;Sessha Muraya Shrine (むらやじんじゃ) : A Shikinaisha. Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi no Kami|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime no Ōkami|1=Himegami}}, {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi no Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, as well as Ōtomo no Takemochi no Muraji and Muroya no Muraji. : Formerly located on the riverside east of the current site at the place name Miyanoyama, but it was destroyed by warfare in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12 and subsequently relocated to the present shrine grounds. ;Massha Hattori Shrine (はっとりじんじゃ) : A Shikinaisha. Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|Q2842779|WD=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mihoko|Q119380365|WD=Q119380365|lt=Ame-no-Mihoko no Mikoto|1=amenomihokonomikoto}}. : Formerly located at Kakimori in Ōaza Daianji, it was destroyed by warfare during the Nanboku-chō period and relocated to the present shrine grounds in Tenshō 1 ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}). ;Sessha Kusumi Shrine (Wakamiya Ebisu Shrine) (くすすみじんじゃ) : A Shikinaisha. Enshrined deities: Ame-no-Kunohi no Mikoto and {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. : Formerly located on the riverside east of the current site at Ebisu-yama, it was relocated to the present shrine grounds in Keiō 2. ;Massha Ichikishimahime Shrine (いちきしまひめじんじゃ) and Mononobe Shrine (もののべじんじゃ) : Enshrined deities: Kashiwaya-hime no Mikoto and Umashimaji no Mikoto; accompanying enshrinement of Mononobe no Moriya no Muraji. Their origins are unknown, but they are said to enshrine the ancestral deities of the shrine families, particularly the Moriya family. Shrine (Ichikishimahime Shrine) === Access === * About a 30-minute walk west from {{ill|lt=Makimuku Station|Q4980794|WD=Q4980794|1=Makimuku Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Sakurai Line|Q865801|WD=Q865801|1=Sakurai Line}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} * About a 40-minute walk east from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|WD=Q7689224|1=Tawaramoto Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} === Notes === 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Muraya-jinja}} [[JA:村屋坐弥冨都比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523074}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine |Image=[[Image:Muraya Jinja Honden.jpg|270px]]
Torii gate and worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}} Kurado 426 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 32 | Latitude seconds = 54.4 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 49 | Longitude seconds = 3.3 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Miho-tsu-hime|Q6378698|WD=Q6378698|lt=Miho-tsu-hime no Mikoto|1=Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Major)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Main hall={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |Alternative name=Muraya Shrine |Founded=Unknown |Map=Japan Nara }} '''Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine''' (むらやにいますみふつひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. The shrine stands facing the “Nakatsu-michi” (Tachibana Highway), one of the {{ill|lt=ancient roads of Yamato|Q11433382|WD=Q11433382|lt=Three Yamato Roads|1=Three Yamato Roads}}. The principal enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Miho-tsu-hime|Q6378698|WD=Q6378698|lt=Miho-tsu-hime no Mikoto|1=Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto}} (also known as Mifutsuhime-gami), with {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi no Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} enshrined as an accompanying deity. Miho-tsu-hime no Mikoto is the consort deity of {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi no Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}, and in the myths recorded in the *Kojiki* and *Nihon Shoki*, Ōmononushi and Ōkuninushi are regarded as the same deity. Ōmononushi no Mikoto is the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}}, and this shrine, which enshrines his consort deity, is considered a branch shrine of Ōmiwa Shrine. ===History=== === Overview === The date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. According to the *{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}*, during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}} in the first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}’s reign ({{ill|lt=673|Q31105|WD=Q31105|1=673}}), the Muraya deity delivered a {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|1=Oracle}} to {{ill|lt=Ōtomo no Fukei|Q11432333|WD=Q11432333|1=Ōtomo no Fukei}}, who was on the side of Prince Ōama, saying, “The enemy is coming, so block the Nakatsu-michi.” In recognition of this service, a {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} was conferred upon the deity. This is the earliest recorded instance of the bestowal of a divine rank. Records such as the Yamato Province Tax Register ({{ill|lt=Shōsōin documents|Q11545362|WD=Q11545362|1=Shōsōin Documents}}) and the {{ill|lt=Shinshōkyakuchokufu-shō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}} note that the imperial court made repeated donations of {{ill|lt=shrine estates|Q1689534|WD=Q1689534|1=Shinpo}} to the shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, the shrine is listed as a major shrine and is recorded as receiving offerings at the Tsukinami, Aename, and Niiname festivals. In Shōwa 2 ({{ill|lt=1926|Q5259|WD=Q5259|1=1926}}), the shrine was elevated to the rank of prefectural shrine. {{quotation| '''“Emperor Tenmu, Upper Volume,” *Nihon Shoki, Lower Volume*, Kodansha Academic Library'''
“Then the deity Muraya (Moriya Shrine) possessed the priest (shinkan) and said, ‘An army will come along the road within my shrine grounds. Therefore, block the shrine road.’” }} === Divine Ranks === * {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japanese era)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (*{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}*) – Recorded as “Muraya Nifutsuhime-gami.” === Shrine Grounds === File:Muraya Jinja Torii1.jpg|First torii gate File:Muraya Jinja Torii2.jpg|Second torii gate File:Muraya Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall === Subsidiary Shrines === ;Sessha Muraya Shrine (むらやじんじゃ) : A Shikinaisha. Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi no Kami|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime no Ōkami|1=Himegami}}, {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi no Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, as well as Ōtomo no Takemochi no Muraji and Muroya no Muraji. : Formerly located on the riverside east of the current site at the place name Miyanoyama, but it was destroyed by warfare in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12 and subsequently relocated to the present shrine grounds. ;Massha Hattori Shrine (はっとりじんじゃ) : A Shikinaisha. Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|Q2842779|WD=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mihoko|Q119380365|WD=Q119380365|lt=Ame-no-Mihoko no Mikoto|1=amenomihokonomikoto}}. : Formerly located at Kakimori in Ōaza Daianji, it was destroyed by warfare during the Nanboku-chō period and relocated to the present shrine grounds in Tenshō 1 ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}). ;Sessha Kusumi Shrine (Wakamiya Ebisu Shrine) (くすすみじんじゃ) : A Shikinaisha. Enshrined deities: Ame-no-Kunohi no Mikoto and {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. : Formerly located on the riverside east of the current site at Ebisu-yama, it was relocated to the present shrine grounds in Keiō 2. ;Massha Ichikishimahime Shrine (いちきしまひめじんじゃ) and Mononobe Shrine (もののべじんじゃ) : Enshrined deities: Kashiwaya-hime no Mikoto and Umashimaji no Mikoto; accompanying enshrinement of Mononobe no Moriya no Muraji. Their origins are unknown, but they are said to enshrine the ancestral deities of the shrine families, particularly the Moriya family. File:Muraya Jinja Sessha1.jpg|Muraya Shrine File:Muraya Jinja Massha1.jpg|Hattori Shrine File:Muraya Jinja Sessha2.jpg|Kusumi Shrine (Wakamiya Ebisu Shrine) File:Muraya Jinja Massha2.jpg|Mononobe Shrine (Ichikishimahime Shrine) === Access === * About a 30-minute walk west from {{ill|lt=Makimuku Station|Q4980794|WD=Q4980794|1=Makimuku Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Sakurai Line|Q865801|WD=Q865801|1=Sakurai Line}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} * About a 40-minute walk east from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|WD=Q7689224|1=Tawaramoto Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related Articles === * {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736|1=Mononobe no Moriya}} === External Links === {{Commonscat|Muraya-jinja}} * [http://www.murayajinja.com Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/temple/3444.html Muraya Shrine] – Tawaramoto Town * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22803 Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Compilation” 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Muraya-jinja}} [[JA:村屋坐弥冨都比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523074}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine |Image=[[Image:Muraya Jinja Honden.jpg|270px]]
Torii gate and worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}} Kurado 426 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 32 | Latitude seconds = 54.4 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 49 | Longitude seconds = 3.3 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Miho-tsu-hime|Q6378698|WD=Q6378698|lt=Miho-tsu-hime no Mikoto|1=Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Major)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Main hall={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |Alternative name=Muraya Shrine |Founded=Unknown |Map=Japan Nara }} '''Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine''' (むらやにいますみふつひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. The shrine stands facing the “Nakatsu-michi” (Tachibana Highway), one of the {{ill|lt=ancient roads of Yamato|Q11433382|WD=Q11433382|lt=Three Yamato Roads|1=Three Yamato Roads}}. The principal enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Miho-tsu-hime|Q6378698|WD=Q6378698|lt=Miho-tsu-hime no Mikoto|1=Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto}} (also known as Mifutsuhime-gami), with {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi no Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} enshrined as an accompanying deity. Miho-tsu-hime no Mikoto is the consort deity of {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi no Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}, and in the myths recorded in the *Kojiki* and *Nihon Shoki*, Ōmononushi and Ōkuninushi are regarded as the same deity. Ōmononushi no Mikoto is the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}}, and this shrine, which enshrines his consort deity, is considered a branch shrine of Ōmiwa Shrine. ===History=== === Overview === The date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. According to the *{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}*, during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}} in the first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}’s reign ({{ill|lt=673|Q31105|WD=Q31105|1=673}}), the Muraya deity delivered a {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|1=Oracle}} to {{ill|lt=Ōtomo no Fukei|Q11432333|WD=Q11432333|1=Ōtomo no Fukei}}, who was on the side of Prince Ōama, saying, “The enemy is coming, so block the Nakatsu-michi.” In recognition of this service, a {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} was conferred upon the deity. This is the earliest recorded instance of the bestowal of a divine rank. Records such as the Yamato Province Tax Register ({{ill|lt=Shōsōin documents|Q11545362|WD=Q11545362|1=Shōsōin Documents}}) and the {{ill|lt=Shinshōkyakuchokufu-shō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}} note that the imperial court made repeated donations of {{ill|lt=shrine estates|Q1689534|WD=Q1689534|1=Shinpo}} to the shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, the shrine is listed as a major shrine and is recorded as receiving offerings at the Tsukinami, Aename, and Niiname festivals. In Shōwa 2 ({{ill|lt=1926|Q5259|WD=Q5259|1=1926}}), the shrine was elevated to the rank of prefectural shrine. {{quotation| '''“Emperor Tenmu, Upper Volume,” *Nihon Shoki, Lower Volume*, Kodansha Academic Library'''
“Then the deity Muraya (Moriya Shrine) possessed the priest (shinkan) and said, ‘An army will come along the road within my shrine grounds. Therefore, block the shrine road.’” }} === Divine Ranks === * {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japanese era)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (*{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}*) – Recorded as “Muraya Nifutsuhime-gami.” === Shrine Grounds === File:Muraya Jinja Torii1.jpg|First torii gate File:Muraya Jinja Torii2.jpg|Second torii gate File:Muraya Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall === Subsidiary Shrines === ;Sessha Muraya Shrine (むらやじんじゃ) : A Shikinaisha. Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi no Kami|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime no Ōkami|1=Himegami}}, {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi no Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, as well as Ōtomo no Takemochi no Muraji and Muroya no Muraji. : Formerly located on the riverside east of the current site at the place name Miyanoyama, but it was destroyed by warfare in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12 and subsequently relocated to the present shrine grounds. ;Massha Hattori Shrine (はっとりじんじゃ) : A Shikinaisha. Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|Q2842779|WD=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mihoko|Q119380365|WD=Q119380365|lt=Ame-no-Mihoko no Mikoto|1=amenomihokonomikoto}}. : Formerly located at Kakimori in Ōaza Daianji, it was destroyed by warfare during the Nanboku-chō period and relocated to the present shrine grounds in Tenshō 1 ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}). ;Sessha Kusumi Shrine (Wakamiya Ebisu Shrine) (くすすみじんじゃ) : A Shikinaisha. Enshrined deities: Ame-no-Kunohi no Mikoto and {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. : Formerly located on the riverside east of the current site at Ebisu-yama, it was relocated to the present shrine grounds in Keiō 2. ;Massha Ichikishimahime Shrine (いちきしまひめじんじゃ) and Mononobe Shrine (もののべじんじゃ) : Enshrined deities: Kashiwaya-hime no Mikoto and Umashimaji no Mikoto; accompanying enshrinement of Mononobe no Moriya no Muraji. Their origins are unknown, but they are said to enshrine the ancestral deities of the shrine families, particularly the Moriya family. File:Muraya Jinja Sessha1.jpg|Muraya Shrine File:Muraya Jinja Massha1.jpg|Hattori Shrine File:Muraya Jinja Sessha2.jpg|Kusumi Shrine (Wakamiya Ebisu Shrine) File:Muraya Jinja Massha2.jpg|Mononobe Shrine (Ichikishimahime Shrine) === Access === * About a 30-minute walk west from {{ill|lt=Makimuku Station|Q4980794|WD=Q4980794|1=Makimuku Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Sakurai Line|Q865801|WD=Q865801|1=Sakurai Line}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} * About a 40-minute walk east from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|WD=Q7689224|1=Tawaramoto Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related Articles === * {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736|1=Mononobe no Moriya}} === External Links === {{Commonscat|Muraya-jinja}} * [http://www.murayajinja.com Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/temple/3444.html Muraya Shrine] – Tawaramoto Town * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22803 Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Compilation” {{Shinto horizontal}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Subshrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大むらやにますみふつひめしんしや]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Muraya-jinja}} [[JA:村屋坐弥冨都比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523074}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine |Image=[[Image:Muraya Jinja Honden.jpg|270px]]
Torii gate and worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}} Kurado 426 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 32 | Latitude seconds = 54.4 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 49 | Longitude seconds = 3.3 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Miho-tsu-hime|Q6378698|WD=Q6378698|lt=Miho-tsu-hime no Mikoto|1=Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Major)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Main hall={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |Alternative name=Muraya Shrine |Founded=Unknown |Map=Japan Nara }} '''Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine''' (むらやにいますみふつひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. The shrine stands facing the “Nakatsu-michi” (Tachibana Highway), one of the {{ill|lt=ancient roads of Yamato|Q11433382|WD=Q11433382|lt=Three Yamato Roads|1=Three Yamato Roads}}. The principal enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Miho-tsu-hime|Q6378698|WD=Q6378698|lt=Miho-tsu-hime no Mikoto|1=Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto}} (also known as Mifutsuhime-gami), with {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi no Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} enshrined as an accompanying deity. Miho-tsu-hime no Mikoto is the consort deity of {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi no Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}, and in the myths recorded in the *Kojiki* and *Nihon Shoki*, Ōmononushi and Ōkuninushi are regarded as the same deity. Ōmononushi no Mikoto is the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}}, and this shrine, which enshrines his consort deity, is considered a branch shrine of Ōmiwa Shrine. ===History=== === Overview === The date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. According to the *{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}*, during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}} in the first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}’s reign ({{ill|lt=673|Q31105|WD=Q31105|1=673}}), the Muraya deity delivered a {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|1=Oracle}} to {{ill|lt=Ōtomo no Fukei|Q11432333|WD=Q11432333|1=Ōtomo no Fukei}}, who was on the side of Prince Ōama, saying, “The enemy is coming, so block the Nakatsu-michi.” In recognition of this service, a {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} was conferred upon the deity. This is the earliest recorded instance of the bestowal of a divine rank. Records such as the Yamato Province Tax Register ({{ill|lt=Shōsōin documents|Q11545362|WD=Q11545362|1=Shōsōin Documents}}) and the {{ill|lt=Shinshōkyakuchokufu-shō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}} note that the imperial court made repeated donations of {{ill|lt=shrine estates|Q1689534|WD=Q1689534|1=Shinpo}} to the shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, the shrine is listed as a major shrine and is recorded as receiving offerings at the Tsukinami, Aename, and Niiname festivals. In Shōwa 2 ({{ill|lt=1926|Q5259|WD=Q5259|1=1926}}), the shrine was elevated to the rank of prefectural shrine. {{quotation| '''“Emperor Tenmu, Upper Volume,” *Nihon Shoki, Lower Volume*, Kodansha Academic Library'''
“Then the deity Muraya (Moriya Shrine) possessed the priest (shinkan) and said, ‘An army will come along the road within my shrine grounds. Therefore, block the shrine road.’” }} === Divine Ranks === * {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japanese era)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (*{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}*) – Recorded as “Muraya Nifutsuhime-gami.” === Shrine Grounds === File:Muraya Jinja Torii1.jpg|First torii gate File:Muraya Jinja Torii2.jpg|Second torii gate File:Muraya Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall === Subsidiary Shrines === ;Sessha Muraya Shrine (むらやじんじゃ) : A Shikinaisha. Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi no Kami|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime no Ōkami|1=Himegami}}, {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi no Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, as well as Ōtomo no Takemochi no Muraji and Muroya no Muraji. : Formerly located on the riverside east of the current site at the place name Miyanoyama, but it was destroyed by warfare in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12 and subsequently relocated to the present shrine grounds. ;Massha Hattori Shrine (はっとりじんじゃ) : A Shikinaisha. Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|Q2842779|WD=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mihoko|Q119380365|WD=Q119380365|lt=Ame-no-Mihoko no Mikoto|1=amenomihokonomikoto}}. : Formerly located at Kakimori in Ōaza Daianji, it was destroyed by warfare during the Nanboku-chō period and relocated to the present shrine grounds in Tenshō 1 ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}). ;Sessha Kusumi Shrine (Wakamiya Ebisu Shrine) (くすすみじんじゃ) : A Shikinaisha. Enshrined deities: Ame-no-Kunohi no Mikoto and {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. : Formerly located on the riverside east of the current site at Ebisu-yama, it was relocated to the present shrine grounds in Keiō 2. ;Massha Ichikishimahime Shrine (いちきしまひめじんじゃ) and Mononobe Shrine (もののべじんじゃ) : Enshrined deities: Kashiwaya-hime no Mikoto and Umashimaji no Mikoto; accompanying enshrinement of Mononobe no Moriya no Muraji. Their origins are unknown, but they are said to enshrine the ancestral deities of the shrine families, particularly the Moriya family. File:Muraya Jinja Sessha1.jpg|Muraya Shrine File:Muraya Jinja Massha1.jpg|Hattori Shrine File:Muraya Jinja Sessha2.jpg|Kusumi Shrine (Wakamiya Ebisu Shrine) File:Muraya Jinja Massha2.jpg|Mononobe Shrine (Ichikishimahime Shrine) === Access === * About a 30-minute walk west from {{ill|lt=Makimuku Station|Q4980794|WD=Q4980794|1=Makimuku Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Sakurai Line|Q865801|WD=Q865801|1=Sakurai Line}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} * About a 40-minute walk east from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|WD=Q7689224|1=Tawaramoto Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related Articles === * {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736|1=Mononobe no Moriya}} === External Links === {{Commonscat|Muraya-jinja}} * [http://www.murayajinja.com Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/temple/3444.html Muraya Shrine] – Tawaramoto Town * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22803 Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Compilation” {{Shinto horizontal}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Muraya-ni-masu-Mifutsuhime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Subshrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:田原本町の建築物]] [[Category:田原本町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Muraya-jinja}} [[JA:村屋坐弥冨都比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523074}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine |Image=[[Image:Muraya Jinja Honden.jpg|270px]]
Torii gate and worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}} Kurado 426 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 32 | Latitude seconds = 54.4 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 49 | Longitude seconds = 3.3 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Miho-tsu-hime|Q6378698|WD=Q6378698|lt=Miho-tsu-hime no Mikoto|1=Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Major)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Main hall={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |Alternative name=Muraya Shrine |Founded=Unknown |Map=Japan Nara }} '''Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine''' (むらやにいますみふつひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. The shrine stands facing the “Nakatsu-michi” (Tachibana Highway), one of the {{ill|lt=ancient roads of Yamato|Q11433382|WD=Q11433382|lt=Three Yamato Roads|1=Three Yamato Roads}}. The principal enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Miho-tsu-hime|Q6378698|WD=Q6378698|lt=Miho-tsu-hime no Mikoto|1=Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto}} (also known as Mifutsuhime-gami), with {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi no Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} enshrined as an accompanying deity. Miho-tsu-hime no Mikoto is the consort deity of {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi no Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}, and in the myths recorded in the *Kojiki* and *Nihon Shoki*, Ōmononushi and Ōkuninushi are regarded as the same deity. Ōmononushi no Mikoto is the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}}, and this shrine, which enshrines his consort deity, is considered a branch shrine of Ōmiwa Shrine. ===History=== === Overview === The date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. According to the *{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}*, during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}} in the first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}’s reign ({{ill|lt=673|Q31105|WD=Q31105|1=673}}), the Muraya deity delivered a {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|1=Oracle}} to {{ill|lt=Ōtomo no Fukei|Q11432333|WD=Q11432333|1=Ōtomo no Fukei}}, who was on the side of Prince Ōama, saying, “The enemy is coming, so block the Nakatsu-michi.” In recognition of this service, a {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} was conferred upon the deity. This is the earliest recorded instance of the bestowal of a divine rank. Records such as the Yamato Province Tax Register ({{ill|lt=Shōsōin documents|Q11545362|WD=Q11545362|1=Shōsōin Documents}}) and the {{ill|lt=Shinshōkyakuchokufu-shō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}} note that the imperial court made repeated donations of {{ill|lt=shrine estates|Q1689534|WD=Q1689534|1=Shinpo}} to the shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, the shrine is listed as a major shrine and is recorded as receiving offerings at the Tsukinami, Aename, and Niiname festivals. In Shōwa 2 ({{ill|lt=1926|Q5259|WD=Q5259|1=1926}}), the shrine was elevated to the rank of prefectural shrine. {{quotation| '''“Emperor Tenmu, Upper Volume,” *Nihon Shoki, Lower Volume*, Kodansha Academic Library'''
“Then the deity Muraya (Moriya Shrine) possessed the priest (shinkan) and said, ‘An army will come along the road within my shrine grounds. Therefore, block the shrine road.’” }} === Divine Ranks === * {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japanese era)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (*{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}*) – Recorded as “Muraya Nifutsuhime-gami.” === Shrine Grounds === File:Muraya Jinja Torii1.jpg|First torii gate File:Muraya Jinja Torii2.jpg|Second torii gate File:Muraya Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall === Subsidiary Shrines === ;Sessha Muraya Shrine (むらやじんじゃ) : A Shikinaisha. Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi no Kami|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime no Ōkami|1=Himegami}}, {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi no Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, as well as Ōtomo no Takemochi no Muraji and Muroya no Muraji. : Formerly located on the riverside east of the current site at the place name Miyanoyama, but it was destroyed by warfare in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12 and subsequently relocated to the present shrine grounds. ;Massha Hattori Shrine (はっとりじんじゃ) : A Shikinaisha. Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|Q2842779|WD=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mihoko|Q119380365|WD=Q119380365|lt=Ame-no-Mihoko no Mikoto|1=amenomihokonomikoto}}. : Formerly located at Kakimori in Ōaza Daianji, it was destroyed by warfare during the Nanboku-chō period and relocated to the present shrine grounds in Tenshō 1 ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}). ;Sessha Kusumi Shrine (Wakamiya Ebisu Shrine) (くすすみじんじゃ) : A Shikinaisha. Enshrined deities: Ame-no-Kunohi no Mikoto and {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. : Formerly located on the riverside east of the current site at Ebisu-yama, it was relocated to the present shrine grounds in Keiō 2. ;Massha Ichikishimahime Shrine (いちきしまひめじんじゃ) and Mononobe Shrine (もののべじんじゃ) : Enshrined deities: Kashiwaya-hime no Mikoto and Umashimaji no Mikoto; accompanying enshrinement of Mononobe no Moriya no Muraji. Their origins are unknown, but they are said to enshrine the ancestral deities of the shrine families, particularly the Moriya family. File:Muraya Jinja Sessha1.jpg|Muraya Shrine File:Muraya Jinja Massha1.jpg|Hattori Shrine File:Muraya Jinja Sessha2.jpg|Kusumi Shrine (Wakamiya Ebisu Shrine) File:Muraya Jinja Massha2.jpg|Mononobe Shrine (Ichikishimahime Shrine) === Access === * About a 30-minute walk west from {{ill|lt=Makimuku Station|Q4980794|WD=Q4980794|1=Makimuku Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Sakurai Line|Q865801|WD=Q865801|1=Sakurai Line}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} * About a 40-minute walk east from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|WD=Q7689224|1=Tawaramoto Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related Articles === * {{ill|lt=Mononobe no Moriya|Q2424736|WD=Q2424736|1=Mononobe no Moriya}} === External Links === {{Commonscat|Muraya-jinja}} * [http://www.murayajinja.com Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/temple/3444.html Muraya Shrine] – Tawaramoto Town * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=22803 Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine] – Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Compilation” 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Muraya-jinja}} [[JA:村屋坐弥冨都比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11523074}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine |Image=[[Image:Muraya Jinja Honden.jpg|270px]]
Torii gate and worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |Location={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}} Kurado 426 | Latitude degrees = 34 | Latitude minutes = 32 | Latitude seconds = 54.4 | N/S = N | Longitude degrees = 135 | Longitude minutes = 49 | Longitude seconds = 3.3 | E/W = E | Map country code = JP |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Miho-tsu-hime|Q6378698|WD=Q6378698|lt=Miho-tsu-hime no Mikoto|1=Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (Major)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Main hall={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |Alternative name=Muraya Shrine |Founded=Unknown |Map=Japan Nara }} '''Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine''' (むらやにいますみふつひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Town|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (major shrine)|Q134917288|WD=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. The shrine stands facing the “Nakatsu-michi” (Tachibana Highway), one of the {{ill|lt=ancient roads of Yamato|Q11433382|WD=Q11433382|lt=Three Yamato Roads|1=Three Yamato Roads}}. The principal enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Miho-tsu-hime|Q6378698|WD=Q6378698|lt=Miho-tsu-hime no Mikoto|1=Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto}} (also known as Mifutsuhime-gami), with {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi no Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} enshrined as an accompanying deity. Miho-tsu-hime no Mikoto is the consort deity of {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi no Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}, and in the myths recorded in the *Kojiki* and *Nihon Shoki*, Ōmononushi and Ōkuninushi are regarded as the same deity. Ōmononushi no Mikoto is the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}}, and this shrine, which enshrines his consort deity, is considered a branch shrine of Ōmiwa Shrine. ===History=== === Overview === The date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. According to the *{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}*, during the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|WD=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}} in the first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenmu|Q335181|WD=Q335181|1=Emperor Tenmu}}’s reign ({{ill|lt=673|Q31105|WD=Q31105|1=673}}), the Muraya deity delivered a {{ill|lt=divine oracle|Q217123|WD=Q217123|1=Oracle}} to {{ill|lt=Ōtomo no Fukei|Q11432333|WD=Q11432333|1=Ōtomo no Fukei}}, who was on the side of Prince Ōama, saying, “The enemy is coming, so block the Nakatsu-michi.” In recognition of this service, a {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} was conferred upon the deity. This is the earliest recorded instance of the bestowal of a divine rank. Records such as the Yamato Province Tax Register ({{ill|lt=Shōsōin documents|Q11545362|WD=Q11545362|1=Shōsōin Documents}}) and the {{ill|lt=Shinshōkyakuchokufu-shō|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}} note that the imperial court made repeated donations of {{ill|lt=shrine estates|Q1689534|WD=Q1689534|1=Shinpo}} to the shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, the shrine is listed as a major shrine and is recorded as receiving offerings at the Tsukinami, Aename, and Niiname festivals. In Shōwa 2 ({{ill|lt=1926|Q5259|WD=Q5259|1=1926}}), the shrine was elevated to the rank of prefectural shrine. {{quotation| '''“Emperor Tenmu, Upper Volume,” *Nihon Shoki, Lower Volume*, Kodansha Academic Library'''
“Then the deity Muraya (Moriya Shrine) possessed the priest (shinkan) and said, ‘An army will come along the road within my shrine grounds. Therefore, block the shrine road.’” }} === Divine Ranks === * {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japanese era)|Q1075611|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|WD=Q2284|1=January 27}}: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (*{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}*) – Recorded as “Muraya Nifutsuhime-gami.” === Shrine Grounds === File:Muraya Jinja Torii1.jpg|First torii gate File:Muraya Jinja Torii2.jpg|Second torii gate File:Muraya Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall === Subsidiary Shrines === ;Sessha Muraya Shrine (むらやじんじゃ) : A Shikinaisha. Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi no Kami|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Hime no Ōkami|1=Himegami}}, {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi no Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, as well as Ōtomo no Takemochi no Muraji and Muroya no Muraji. : Formerly located on the riverside east of the current site at the place name Miyanoyama, but it was destroyed by warfare in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 12 and subsequently relocated to the present shrine grounds. ;Massha Hattori Shrine (はっとりじんじゃ) : A Shikinaisha. Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|Q2842779|WD=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Mihoko|Q119380365|WD=Q119380365|lt=Ame-no-Mihoko no Mikoto|1=amenomihokonomikoto}}. : Formerly located at Kakimori in Ōaza Daianji, it was destroyed by warfare during the Nanboku-chō period and relocated to the present shrine grounds in Tenshō 1 ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|WD=Q6678|1=1573}}). ;Sessha Kusumi Shrine (Wakamiya Ebisu Shrine) (くすすみじんじゃ) : A Shikinaisha. Enshrined deities: Ame-no-Kunohi no Mikoto and {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}. : Formerly located on the riverside east of the current site at Ebisu-yama, it was relocated to the present shrine grounds in Keiō 2. ;Massha Ichikishimahime Shrine (いちきしまひめじんじゃ) and Mononobe Shrine (もののべじんじゃ) : Enshrined deities: Kashiwaya-hime no Mikoto and Umashimaji no Mikoto; accompanying enshrinement of Mononobe no Moriya no Muraji. Their origins are unknown, but they are said to enshrine the ancestral deities of the shrine families, particularly the Moriya family. File:Muraya Jinja Sessha1.jpg|Muraya Shrine File:Muraya Jinja Massha1.jpg|Hattori Shrine File:Muraya Jinja Sessha2.jpg|Kusumi Shrine (Wakamiya Ebisu Shrine) File:Muraya Jinja Massha2.jpg|Mononobe Shrine (Ichikishimahime Shrine) === Access === * About a 30-minute walk west from {{ill|lt=Makimuku Station|Q4980794|WD=Q4980794|1=Makimuku Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Sakurai Line|Q865801|WD=Q865801|1=Sakurai Line}} of {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} * About a 40-minute walk east from {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto Station|Q7689224|WD=Q7689224|1=Tawaramoto Station}} on the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashihara Line|1=UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Kintetsu|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} === Notes === Muraya-niimasu-Mifutsuhime Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.town.tawaramoto.nara.jp/kanko/bunkazai/temple/3444.html Muraya Shrine] – Tawaramoto Town *
